rationale: The cited 2000 paper is a close conceptual precedent: it examines how negative brand information affects attitudes with moderation by consumer–brand bond strength (commitment), while the 2013 citing paper studies brand-attitude dilution from conspicuous use moderated by self-brand connection. Given this overlap in core outcome (brand attitude) and moderator logic/mechanism framing, this is not an obvious incidental mention, even though the raw evidence only confirms a DOI-level match.
